rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by using only visible identifiers from the current component scope
Ensure AGREE expressions match the expected types (bool, int, real) and avoid <error> type issues
Move implementation-level bindings to implementation annexes and avoid type scope mismatches
Delete invalid AGREE clauses that reference undefined or inaccessible identifiers
generic_rules: Validate all AGREE references against the visible AADL model structure before repair
Preserve type consistency in AGREE expressions to prevent diagnostics about mismatched types
Use only declared helpers or visible features in AGREE contracts to avoid resolution errors
Avoid introducing new identifiers or types not present in the original AADL model
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid annex scopes (component type or implementation)
Helper functions and constants must be declared with correct scalar types (int, real, bool)
Property associations and references must resolve to valid AADL identifiers in scope
Assume/guarantee expressions must evaluate to boolean types
anti_patterns: Do not add new package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the model
Do not attempt to fix unresolved references by inventing new declarations
Do not mix component type and implementation scope references in the same contract
Do not convert Boolean ports to numeric comparisons unless explicitly supported by AADL
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
AGREE validator diagnostics about annex placement
Model Analyst Agent's role in extracting component structure
Requirement Analyst Agent's role in traceability
